bank: Headline with emotionally charged words gets more clicks than the neutral headline
Headline using numbers or statistics attracts more clicks than a headline without them
Headline phrased as a question generates more clicks than a declarative headline
Headline including a direct call to action receives more clicks than a passive headline
Headline with shorter length (fewer words) gains more clicks than a longer headline
Headline containing superlative adjectives (e.g., best, biggest) gets more clicks than a plain headline
Headline that teases a mystery or surprise results in more clicks than a straightforward headline
Headline using active voice has more clicks than a headline in passive voice
Headline mentioning a well-known person or brand draws more clicks than a generic headline
Headline with informal or conversational tone attracts more clicks than a formal tone headline
Headline emphasizing urgency or time sensitivity gets more clicks than a neutral timing headline
Headline employing humor or wordplay receives more clicks than a serious headline
Headline that highlights personal benefit or relevance brings more clicks than a general headline
Headline with keywords optimized for search engines generates more clicks than a non-optimized headline
Headline that includes a clear value proposition or promise gets more clicks than a vague headline
